steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content:
The sentence is: “Lai and Muñoz recognize the need for direct observation [blank] recommending evolving binary star systems as a promising avenue for future study.”

The key question: is “recommending evolving binary star systems...” an independent clause?

No—it has no finite verb and no stated subject. It's a participial phrase continuing the description within the same clause.

This is ONE clause, not two.

steps.title: Apply the rule for parenthetical elements
steps.content:
Since “however” is inserted within a single clause to provide contrast or qualification, it functions as a parenthetical element. Parenthetical elements take commas on both sides: “...observation, however, recommending...”

Choice A (‘observation, however,’) correctly places commas around “however” to mark it as a parenthetical interjection. The other choices incorrectly treat “however” as a transition between independent clauses; since “recommending” is not an independent clause, those patterns don't apply. An incorrect response violates standard punctuation for parenthetical elements by placing a semicolon after “however.”

That confirms A is correct.
